# pip-audit ignore list (nousergon/alpha-engine-config#2342)
# Every entry below MUST carry a dated rationale comment directly above it.
# No blanket suppression — each accepted advisory is named individually.
# Format:
#   # <rationale> — accepted 2026-07-12, revisit <YYYY-MM-DD or "when a fix is released">
#   <VULN-ID>

# langchain 0.3.30 -> fix requires langchain 1.3.9. The fix version is on
# the langchain "1.0" line, an ecosystem-wide rename/rewrite (langchain,
# langchain-openai, langchain-text-splitters, langgraph, langgraph-checkpoint,
# langgraph-sdk all did coordinated 0.x -> 1.x major bumps together).
# langchain here is a transitive dep of agentevals (via requirements.txt's
# agentevals~=0.0), not a direct import of ours. Verified locally: forcing
# langgraph==1.0.10 alone drags in langchain-core 1.4.9, which breaks the
# langchain-anthropic~=0.3 pin this repo's production research pipeline
# depends on (langchain-anthropic 0.3.22 requires langchain-core<1.0.0).
# Adopting the fix means validating the whole langchain-1.0 migration
# (breaking message/tool-call API changes) across graph/, thinktank/, and
# evals/ — too large to safely land in this pip-audit-blocking PR.
# Path-confinement issue (file-search agent middleware, prompt/config
# loaders); this repo doesn't use langchain's file-search agent middleware
# or path-taking config loaders.
# accepted 2026-07-12, revisit when nousergon/alpha-engine-config#2342's
# fleet sweep is followed by a dedicated langchain-1.0 migration ticket.
CVE-2026-55443

# langchain-openai 0.3.35 -> fix requires 1.1.14 (same langchain-1.0 line,
# see CVE-2026-55443 above; transitive via agentevals). SSRF/TOCTOU issue is
# in _url_to_size() image-token-counting helper; this repo doesn't pass
# untrusted image URLs through langchain-openai's token counting.
# accepted 2026-07-12, revisit when nousergon/alpha-engine-config#2342's
# fleet sweep is followed by a dedicated langchain-1.0 migration ticket.
PYSEC-2026-76

# langchain-text-splitters 0.3.11 -> fix requires 1.1.2 (same langchain-1.0
# line; transitive via both langchain and voyageai). SSRF-via-redirect issue
# is in HTMLHeaderTextSplitter.split_text_from_url(), which this repo does
# not call (no URL-fetching text splitter usage found).
# accepted 2026-07-12, revisit when nousergon/alpha-engine-config#2342's
# fleet sweep is followed by a dedicated langchain-1.0 migration ticket.
PYSEC-2026-77

# langgraph 0.6.11 -> fix requires 1.0.10 (major bump off the pinned
# langgraph~=0.2 range; confirmed pulls langchain-core 1.x which breaks
# langchain-anthropic~=0.3, see CVE-2026-55443 above for the full
# cross-package dependency proof). Unsafe checkpoint deserialization is a
# defense-in-depth issue requiring an attacker to already have write access
# to the checkpoint backing store (post-exploitation scenario) — not
# remotely exploitable on its own.
# accepted 2026-07-12, revisit when nousergon/alpha-engine-config#2342's
# fleet sweep is followed by a dedicated langchain-1.0/langgraph-1.0
# migration ticket.
PYSEC-2026-83

# langgraph-checkpoint 3.0.1 -> fix requires 4.0.0 (transitive from
# langgraph~=0.2; same migration blocker as PYSEC-2026-83 above). RCE via
# pickle_fallback in BaseCache requires the application to explicitly
# enable a langgraph cache backend with CachePolicy — this repo does not
# use langgraph's caching layer (no `cache=` passed to StateGraph).
# accepted 2026-07-12, revisit when nousergon/alpha-engine-config#2342's
# fleet sweep is followed by a dedicated langchain-1.0/langgraph-1.0
# migration ticket.
CVE-2026-27794

# langgraph-checkpoint 3.0.1 -> fix requires 4.1.1 (transitive from
# langgraph~=0.2; same migration blocker as PYSEC-2026-83/CVE-2026-27794
# above). JsonPlusSerializer object reconstruction is defense-in-depth,
# reachable only if checkpoint bytes at rest are already attacker-modified
# (implies a prior serious compromise of the backing store).
# accepted 2026-07-12, revisit when nousergon/alpha-engine-config#2342's
# fleet sweep is followed by a dedicated langchain-1.0/langgraph-1.0
# migration ticket.
CVE-2026-48775

# langgraph-sdk 0.2.15 -> fix requires 0.3.15 (transitive from
# langgraph~=0.2; same migration blocker as above). URL-path-injection via
# unsanitized identifiers affects the langgraph-sdk HTTP client talking to
# a LangGraph Platform deployment; this repo does not use langgraph-sdk's
# client against an untrusted/multi-tenant LangGraph Platform deployment.
# accepted 2026-07-12, revisit when nousergon/alpha-engine-config#2342's
# fleet sweep is followed by a dedicated langchain-1.0/langgraph-1.0
# migration ticket.
CVE-2026-48776

# pytest 8.4.2 -> fix requires 9.0.3, which is a major bump off the pinned
# pytest~=8.2 range. Verified locally: pytest 9.x forces pytest-asyncio to
# also jump 0.x -> 1.x (pytest-asyncio 0.26.0, our current resolved
# version, requires pytest<9; only pytest-asyncio>=1.0.0 supports pytest 9),
# compounding into a second breaking bump. The vulnerability itself
# (PYSEC-2026-1845) is a local-only /tmp/pytest-of-{user} directory-naming
# DoS/privilege issue requiring a malicious co-tenant on the same
# multi-user host — not applicable to GitHub Actions' single-tenant runners
# or CI's threat model.
# accepted 2026-07-12, revisit when a pytest-asyncio 1.x migration is
# planned separately (dev-only dependency, not shipped to production).
PYSEC-2026-1845

# langchain-core 0.3.86 -> fix requires 1.2.22 (same langchain-1.0 line as
# CVE-2026-55443 above; langchain-core is transitive via langchain-anthropic~=0.3
# + agentevals~=0.0, not a direct dep). Adopting 1.2.22 pulls langchain-core onto
# the 1.x line, which breaks the langchain-anthropic~=0.3 pin this repo's research
# pipeline depends on (langchain-anthropic 0.3.22 requires langchain-core<1.0.0) —
# the identical migration blocker proven under CVE-2026-55443. The advisory itself
# is a directory-traversal in langchain_core.prompts.loading.load_prompt() /
# load_prompt_from_config() when passed user-influenced prompt configs; this repo
# does not use that surface — its own agents.prompt_loader.load_prompt(name) takes
# fixed prompt names, and there is no langchain_core.prompts.loading import.
# accepted 2026-07-13, revisit when nousergon/alpha-engine-config#2342's fleet
# sweep is followed by a dedicated langchain-1.0 migration ticket.
PYSEC-2026-2193

# langchain-openai 1.x -> SSRF via image_url token counting in ChatOpenAI.get_num_tokens_from_messages().
# Fix requires langchain-openai>=1.1.9 (langchain-core>=1.2.11) — the same langchain-1.0
# line as CVE-2026-55443/PYSEC-2026-76 above; langchain-anthropic~=0.3's langchain-core<1.0.0
# constraint blocks the upgrade identically. This repo does not call get_num_tokens_from_messages()
# with untrusted image URLs — the SSRF vector requires attacker-controlled image_url content
# in the message, which is never present in this repo's agent/chain invocations (fixed prompt
# structures, no user-supplied image URLs).
# accepted 2026-07-24, revisit when the dedicated langchain-1.0 migration ticket is completed.
PYSEC-2026-2562
